content.problemStatement:
Given an array of daily stock prices, calculate the span for each day. The span of a stock price on day iii is defined as the maximum number of consecutive days just before day iii (including day iii) for which the stock price is less than or equal to the price on day iii.


Input Format

First line contains NNN, the number of days
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing daily stock prices


Output Format

Print NNN space-separated integers representing the span for each day.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Day 1 (100): span = 1 (no previous days) 
Day 2 (80): span = 1 (can't include 100) 
Day 3 (60): span = 1 (can't include 80) 
Day 4 (70): span = 2 (includes 60) 
Day 5 (60): span = 1 (can't include 70) 
Day 6 (75): span = 4 (includes 60,70,60) 
Day 7 (85): span = 6 (includes 75,60,70,60,80)

Explanation:
Day 1 (10): span = 1 (no previous days) 
Day 2 (4): span = 1 (can't include 10) 
Day 3 (5): span = 2 (includes 4) 
Day 4 (90): span = 4 (includes 5,4,10) 
Day 5 (120): span = 5 (includes all previous) 
Day 6 (80): span = 1 (can't include 120)
